learning_outcomes.description: Understand and apply the thermodynamic principles relating to intermolecular and interfacial forces
Understand and apply fundamental aspects of polymer structure, synthesis and characterisation and to appreciate their applications in materials science
Understand and apply the principles of molecular spectroscopy.
Understand and apply the periodic properties of the main group elements and appreciate how relationships explain the structures and properties of their compounds

Demonstrate acquisition of, and proficiency in: laboratory skills and techniques; data processing and display; critical appraisal of experimental outcomes related to concepts taught in the course. Communication of experimental observations and outcomes.
Analyse and solve problems through the recognition of principles taught in lectures and the application of both quantitative and critical reasoning skills.

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Notebook/ Logbook, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation
assessments.task_description:
The laboratory assessment in CHEM2902 comprises:

Three laboratory activities that are common to CHEM2060 and an extended experimental experience that is unique to CHEM2902

  • Laboratory Preparation Workshop (Pass/Fail) July 31st
  • Dry Laboratory Workshop: Observations and Data analysis task sheet (5%) September 18th, assessment submitted during workshop
  • Experiment 1 Report (7.5%) (Due 13/8/2026)
  • CHEM2902 Extended Laboratory Experience Individual Report (15%) (Due 16/9/2026)
  • Group Oral Presentation (2.5%) (7/10/2026)


The due date for submission of reports is at 5:00 pm, 1 week after your experiment has been scheduled, details will be provided in the laboratory folder on Bb.

Completion of all the laboratory activities and assessment is a hurdle in this course. A printed laboratory manual for the laboratory activities will be provided to you in the first session and will also be available digitally in Bb - you are required to read the laboratory notes and complete pre-lab tasks prior to coming to the laboratory. Protective glasses, appropriate footwear and a laboratory coat are essential and must be worn in the laboratory.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Laboratory Report through the course Turnitin submission point in the relevant experiment folder in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>10%  Calculated from the average of all workshops </dd>

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
There are five workshop sessions that involve submission of an assessment task, one from each module taught plus the laboratory workshop in Week 1. Students will need to complete their submission within 1 hour after the end time of the assigned contact session.

This is a Pass/Fail assessment where 4 out of a possible 5 workshop submissions that meet the task criteria are required.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The completed task materials need to be submitted either online or in person at the end of the contact session (no coversheet is required).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This will be a 60 minute closed book, in-semester invigilated exam scheduled during Week 7 of semester, the exact time and venue will be announced through Bb and an email to students. Students will be permitted to bring one A4 page (double-sided) of handwritten notes into the examination and this will be collected with the exam paper at the end of the session.

This exam will address topics covered during Modules 1 & 2 (Spectroscopy and Polymer Chemistry).

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This will be a 120 minute closed book, invigilated exam where students will be permitted to bring one A4 page of double-sided, handwritten notes into the examination. The notes will be collected with the exam paper at the end of the session.

Concepts taught in modules 3 and 4 of the course (Thermodynamics and Main Group chemistry) will be examined.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
